# py-ethclient

> A Python L2 development platform for building application-specific ZK rollups. Define your State Transition Function as a plain Python function — the framework handles sequencing, batching, Groth16 proving, and L1 verification. Built on a fully independent Ethereum L1 execution client with EVM, RLPx, eth/68, snap/1, full+snap sync, Engine API, L1↔L2 bridge, and Groth16 ZK toolkit — all in pure Python.

py-ethclient is the only Ethereum execution client written in Python. It is a fully independent implementation inspired by ethrex (Rust), connecting directly to the Ethereum peer-to-peer network via devp2p/RLPx. The project contains 22,050 lines of source code across 88 files with 1,031 tests covering all protocol layers.

## Why py-ethclient?

- Application-Specific L2 Development: Write your rollup logic as a plain Python function. The framework handles sequencing, batching, Groth16 proving, and L1 verification. No Solidity, no circom, no complex toolchains — just Python.
- ZK Circuit Development: Define circuits in Python, generate proofs, and test on-chain verification in a single process. The fastest way to prototype ZK applications.
- Education & Research: Python's readability makes it the best codebase for understanding how Ethereum works at the protocol level.
- Rapid Prototyping: Test new EIPs, custom opcodes, or consensus changes in hours instead of days.
- Client Diversity: Adds a Python client alongside Go (geth), Rust (reth), C# (Nethermind), and Java (Besu).

## Comparison with Other Clients

| Client | Language | Purpose | App-Specific Rollup | ZK Proving | L2 Bridge | EVM | Sync | Engine API | P2P |
|--------|----------|---------|---------------------|------------|-----------|-----|------|------------|-----|
| py-ethclient | Python | L2 Dev, ZK, Education | Built-in | Groth16 | CrossDomainMessenger | 140+ opcodes | Full + Snap | V1/V2/V3 | eth/68, snap/1 |
| geth | Go | Production | N/A | N/A | N/A | Full | Full + Snap + Light | V1/V2/V3 | eth/68, snap/1 |
| reth | Rust | Production | N/A | N/A | N/A | Full | Full + Snap | V1/V2/V3 | eth/68, snap/1 |
| Nethermind | C# | Production | N/A | N/A | N/A | Full | Full + Snap + Fast | V1/V2/V3 | eth/68, snap/1 |

## L2 Rollup Framework (ethclient/l2/)

The L2 module provides a complete application-specific ZK rollup framework with 4 pluggable interfaces:

- [Rollup Orchestrator](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/rollup.py): Main user-facing API. Constructor accepts STF (callable, StateTransitionFunction, or None), optional DA, L1, prover, config. Auto-wraps callables in PythonRuntime. Defaults: LocalDAProvider, InMemoryL1Backend, Groth16ProofBackend. Methods: setup() (trusted setup + verifier deployment), submit_tx(tx), produce_batch() → Batch, prove_and_submit(batch) → BatchReceipt, chain_info() → dict. Properties: state, state_root, is_setup.

- [Types](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/types.py): L2TxType(IntEnum) — CALL=0, DEPOSIT=1, WITHDRAWAL=2. L2Tx dataclass — sender, nonce, data (dict), value, tx_type, signature, timestamp. Methods: tx_hash(), encode(), decode(). L2State(dict) — snapshot() for deep copy. STFResult — success, output, error. Batch — number, transactions, old_state_root, new_state_root, da_commitment, proof, status flags (sealed/proven/submitted/verified). BatchReceipt — batch_number, l1_tx_hash, verified, state_root. Uses RLP encoding with tag-based dict serialization.

- [Config](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/config.py): L2Config dataclass — name ("py-rollup"), chain_id (42170), max_txs_per_batch (64), batch_timeout (10s), sequencer_address, genesis_state, rpc_port (9545).

- [Interfaces](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/interfaces.py): 4 abstract base classes. StateTransitionFunction — apply_tx(state, tx) → STFResult, validate_tx() returns None, genesis_state() returns {}. DAProvider — store_batch(), retrieve_batch(), verify_commitment(). L1Backend — deploy_verifier(vk), submit_batch(), is_batch_verified(), get_verified_state_root(). ProofBackend — setup(), prove(), verify(), verification_key property.

- [State Store](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/state.py): L2StateStore — manages L2 state with Merkle state root via Trie. Methods: compute_state_root(), snapshot() → int, rollback(snapshot_id), commit(). Tag-based encoding: 0x01=int, 0x02=str, 0x03=dict, 0x04=list.

- [Runtime](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/runtime.py): PythonRuntime(StateTransitionFunction) — wraps a plain Python function as an STF. Constructor takes func, optional validator, optional genesis. apply_tx() catches exceptions → STFResult(success=False).

- [DA Provider](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/da.py): LocalDAProvider(DAProvider) — in-memory dict. Commitment = keccak256(batch_number_bytes || data).

- [Sequencer](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/sequencer.py): Sequencer — sequences transactions, executes STF, assembles batches. Methods: submit_tx(tx) → validates, checks nonce. tick() → processes mempool with snapshot/rollback. force_seal() → Optional[Batch]. Auto-seals at max_txs_per_batch.

- [Prover](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/prover.py): Groth16ProofBackend(ProofBackend) — 128-bit truncation for BN128 scalar field safety. Circuit: 3 public (old_state_root, new_state_root, tx_commitment), 3 private (old_state_hash, new_state_hash, delta). 3 constraints: (1) binding old, (2) binding new, (3) transition integrity.

- [L1 Backend](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/l1_backend.py): InMemoryL1Backend(L1Backend) — simulates L1 verification using groth16.verify directly. deploy_verifier() stores VK, returns deterministic address. submit_batch() verifies proof, tracks verified batches.

- [Submitter](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/submitter.py): BatchSubmitter — orchestrates prove → submit → verify. process_batch(batch) → BatchReceipt.

- [L2 RPC API](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/rpc_api.py): register_l2_api(rpc, rollup) — 7 methods: l2_sendTransaction, l2_getState, l2_getStateRoot, l2_getBatch, l2_produceBatch, l2_proveAndSubmit, l2_chainInfo.

- [L2 CLI](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/cli.py): Subcommand "l2" with init (scaffolds l2.json + stf.py), start, prove, submit.

- [S3 DA Provider](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/da_s3.py): S3DAProvider(DAProvider) — stores batch data in S3 bucket. Configurable bucket, prefix, region, endpoint_url. Commitment = keccak256(batch_number_8bytes || data). Requires boto3 (optional dependency).

- [Calldata DA Provider](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/da_calldata.py): CalldataDAProvider(DAProvider) — posts batch data as calldata in EIP-1559 (type-2) L1 transactions. Uses EthRPCClient for JSON-RPC transport, ecdsa_sign for signing. Commitment = keccak256(batch_number_8bytes || data). Configurable receipt_timeout.

- [Blob DA Provider](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/da_blob.py): BlobDAProvider(DAProvider) — stores batch data as EIP-4844 blobs on L1. Blob encoding: 4-byte length header + data packed into 31-byte chunks (high byte = 0x00 for BLS modulus safety). 4096 field elements per blob, max ~126,972 bytes per blob. Uses KZG commitments via ckzg trusted setup.

- [Native Prover](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/native_prover.py): NativeProverBackend(ProofBackend) — subprocess-based Groth16 proving via rapidsnark/snarkjs CLI. Witness computation in Python, proof generation delegated to native binaries. Working directory: circuit.r1cs, witness.json, proving_key.zkey, proof.json. Falls back to Python prover if native binaries unavailable. Uses R1CS export and snarkjs compat for format interop.

- [Real L1 Backend](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/eth_l1_backend.py): EthL1Backend(L1Backend) — real Ethereum L1 backend via JSON-RPC. Deploys Groth16 verifier contract on-chain, submits batch proofs as EIP-1559 transactions, verifies on-chain via EVMVerifier calldata encoding. Configurable gas_multiplier, receipt_timeout. Uses EthRPCClient for transport.

- [Persistent State](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/persistent_state.py): L2PersistentState(dict) — LMDB-backed state with overlay pattern. STF writes to overlay dict, reads fall through to LMDB. flush() atomically commits overlay to disk. L2PersistentStateStore — manages state root via Trie, persists batches, proofs, nonces, and metadata. WAL (write-ahead log) for crash recovery. Transparent dict interface for STF compatibility.

- [Eth RPC Client](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/eth_rpc.py): EthRPCClient — lightweight Ethereum JSON-RPC client using urllib (no external dependencies). Methods: get_nonce, send_raw_transaction, get_transaction_receipt, get_gas_price, etc. EthRPCError for JSON-RPC/transport failures.

- [Metrics Collector](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/metrics.py): L2MetricsCollector — collects operational metrics from the rollup. Tracks mempool size, sealed/proven/submitted batch counts, tx submitted/failed totals, prove/submit durations (average).

- [Health Endpoints](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/health.py): register_health_endpoints(app, rollup) — /health returns {"status": "ok"}, /ready returns readiness with chain_id, state_root, pending_txs, sealed_batches (503 if not setup). /metrics endpoint registered in CLI via L2MetricsCollector.

- [Middleware](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/l2/middleware.py): APIKeyMiddleware — X-API-Key header validation, bypasses /health /ready /metrics. RateLimitMiddleware — IP-based token bucket rate limiting with configurable capacity and rate. RequestSizeLimitMiddleware — rejects oversized request bodies (default 1MB). All Starlette BaseHTTPMiddleware.

## ZK Toolkit (ethclient/zk/)

- [Circuit Builder](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/zk/circuit.py): Circuit, Signal, R1CS classes. Python operator overloading for R1CS definition. Signal __mul__ auto-creates constraints. constrain(lhs, rhs) optimizes. compute_witness() via constraint propagation. Field: BN128 curve_order.

- [Groth16 Prover](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/zk/groth16.py): setup(circuit) → (ProvingKey, VerificationKey) via QAP + toxic waste. prove(pk, private, public, circuit) → Proof with A/B/C commitments. verify(vk, proof, public_inputs) → bool via pairing check. debug_verify() → DebugResult with individual pairing values. QAP: Lagrange interpolation, polynomial arithmetic in BN128 scalar field.

- [EVM Verifier](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/zk/evm_verifier.py): EVMVerifier(vk) — auto-generates EVM bytecode (~850 bytes) using ecMul/ecAdd/ecPairing precompiles. verify_on_evm(proof, inputs) → result. gas_profile() → per-precompile breakdown.

- [snarkjs Compat](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/zk/snarkjs_compat.py): Round-trip import/export of snarkjs vkey.json and proof.json. G2 FQ2 order: snarkjs uses [imaginary, real] (c1, c0).

- [ZK Types](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/zk/types.py): G1Point, G2Point, Proof, VerificationKey, ProvingKey dataclasses.

- [Poseidon Circuit](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/zk/poseidon_circuit.py): ZK-friendly Poseidon hash R1CS encoding. poseidon_circuit(c, inputs) → Signal. Uses S-box x^5 (3 constraints each), MDS matrix mixing (linear, 0 constraints), round constant addition (linear). Parameters: t=3, RF=8 full rounds, RP=57 partial rounds. ~240 R1CS constraints vs ~150,000 for keccak256. Enables in-proof Merkle tree verification.

- [R1CS Export](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/zk/r1cs_export.py): R1CS binary format export for snarkjs/circom toolchain compatibility. export_r1cs_binary(circuit) → bytes (.r1cs binary with header, constraints, wire mapping sections). export_witness_json(circuit, witness) → JSON for snarkjs/rapidsnark. Format follows iden3 r1cs_bin_format spec.

## L1↔L2 Bridge (ethclient/bridge/)

- [CrossDomainMessenger](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/bridge/messenger.py): Optimism-style messenger. send_message() → outbox. relay_message() → execute on target EVM. Pluggable relay handlers. Replay protection via message hash tracking. Force inclusion queue with configurable window (50 blocks). Escape hatch for L1 value recovery.

- [Relay Handlers](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/bridge/relay_handlers.py): RelayHandler ABC + 5 implementations. EVMRelayHandler (default, full EVM execution), MerkleProofHandler (Merkle proof against trusted L1 root), ZKProofHandler (Groth16 verification), TinyDBHandler (document DB backend), DirectStateHandler (trusted relayer).

- [Bridge Watcher](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/bridge/watcher.py): BridgeWatcher — automated outbox drain, bidirectional message relay, force queue processing.

- [Bridge Environment](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/bridge/environment.py): BridgeEnvironment — L1+L2 stores, 2 messengers, watcher. Factory methods: with_direct_state(), with_merkle_proof(), with_zk_proof(vk). Helper methods: send_l1/l2, relay, force_include, force_relay, escape_hatch, l1/l2_balance, l2_storage.

- [Bridge Types](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/bridge/types.py): CrossDomainMessage, RelayResult, ForceInclusionEntry, StateUpdate.

## Core Modules (ethclient/common/)

- [RLP Encoding](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/common/rlp.py): Recursive Length Prefix encoding/decoding — Ethereum's core serialization format. Handles both bytes and nested lists with automatic length discrimination.

- [Types](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/common/types.py): Block, BlockHeader (21 RLP fields post-Prague), Transaction (Legacy/EIP-2930/EIP-1559/EIP-4844/EIP-7702), Account, Receipt, Log. Supports all transaction types through Prague hardfork.

- [Merkle Patricia Trie](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/common/trie.py): Full implementation with Branch/Extension/Leaf nodes, hex-prefix encoding, state root computation. Includes proof generation (prove), verification (verify_proof), and range proof verification (verify_range_proof) for snap sync.

- [Crypto](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/common/crypto.py): Keccak-256 (NOT SHA-3/256 — Ethereum uses pre-FIPS Keccak with different padding), secp256k1 ECDSA sign/recover, address derivation. Includes KeccakState wrapper for incremental hashing.

- [Poseidon Hash](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/common/hash.py): ZK-friendly Poseidon hash over BN128 scalar field. Sponge construction with t=3 (state width), rate=2, capacity=1, alpha=5 (S-box exponent), RF=8 full rounds, RP=57 partial rounds. Round constants via SHA-256 counter mode, MDS matrix via Cauchy construction. poseidon(inputs: list[int]) → int (field elements), poseidon_bytes(data: bytes) → bytes (31-byte chunks → 32-byte output). Pluggable into Trie via hash_fn parameter for ZK-friendly Merkle trees.

- [Chain Config](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/common/config.py): Network configurations for Mainnet, Sepolia, Holesky. Hardfork parameters, genesis block handling, ForkID (EIP-2124) computation.

## EVM (ethclient/vm/)

- [EVM Core](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/vm/evm.py): Main execution loop with execute_transaction() for full tx processing and execute_message() for internal calls. Handles contract creation (CREATE/CREATE2), value transfers, and gas management. simulate_call() for eth_call/eth_estimateGas (snapshot → execute → rollback).

- [Opcodes](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/vm/opcodes.py): All opcode handlers — arithmetic (ADD, MUL, SUB, DIV, MOD, EXP, SIGNEXTEND), comparison (LT, GT, EQ, ISZERO), bitwise (AND, OR, XOR, NOT, SHL, SHR, SAR), stack (PUSH1-32, DUP1-16, SWAP1-16, POP), memory (MLOAD, MSTORE, MSTORE8, MSIZE, MCOPY), storage (SLOAD, SSTORE with EIP-2200/3529 gas), control flow (JUMP, JUMPI, JUMPDEST), logging (LOG0-4), system (CALL, DELEGATECALL, STATICCALL, CREATE, CREATE2, SELFDESTRUCT, RETURN, REVERT), block context (BLOCKHASH, COINBASE, TIMESTAMP, NUMBER, PREVRANDAO, GASLIMIT, CHAINID, BASEFEE, BLOBHASH, BLOBBASEFEE).

- [Precompiles](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/vm/precompiles.py): 0x01 ecrecover, 0x02 SHA-256, 0x03 RIPEMD-160, 0x04 identity, 0x05 modexp (EIP-2565), 0x06 ecAdd (BN128), 0x07 ecMul (BN128), 0x08 ecPairing (BN128), 0x09 BLAKE2f (EIP-152), 0x0A KZG point evaluation (EIP-4844).

- [Gas Calculation](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/vm/gas.py): EIP-2929 cold/warm access tracking. EIP-2200 + EIP-3529 SSTORE gas rules with refund calculation. Memory expansion cost. Dynamic gas for CALL variants.

- [Memory & Stack](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/vm/memory.py): 256-bit stack with 1024 depth limit, byte-addressable memory with automatic expansion.

- [Call Frames](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/vm/call_frame.py): Call frame management for CALL/DELEGATECALL/STATICCALL. JUMPDEST validation and analysis caching.

- [Execution Hooks](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/vm/hooks.py): L2 extensibility with before_execution, before_call, and on_state_change hooks.

## Blockchain Engine (ethclient/blockchain/)

- [Chain](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/blockchain/chain.py): Block validation pipeline — header validation (parent hash, number, timestamp, gas limit, base fee per EIP-1559), transaction execution (signature verification, nonce check, balance deduction, gas accounting, receipt generation), state root verification, simulate_call() for read-only EVM execution.

- [Mempool](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/blockchain/mempool.py): Transaction pool with nonce-ordered queuing, fee-based priority, and replacement policy.

- [Fork Choice](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/blockchain/fork_choice.py): Canonical chain management with reorganization support.

## Storage (ethclient/storage/)

- [Store Interface](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/storage/store.py): Abstract base class. Methods for accounts, storage slots, code, blocks, transaction indexing, and snap sync operations. Convenience methods always go through put_account().

- [Memory Backend](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/storage/memory_backend.py): In-memory implementation using dicts. State root via Merkle Patricia Trie.

- [Disk Backend](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/storage/disk_backend.py): LMDB-backed with 13 named databases. Hybrid overlay pattern — state writes to overlay, flush() atomically commits. Block data writes directly. Activated with --data-dir.

## P2P Networking (ethclient/networking/)

- [P2P Server](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/server.py): Multi-protocol dispatch, peer connection management.

- [RLPx Handshake](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/rlpx/handshake.py): ECIES encrypted handshake (EIP-8). Random padding, 2-byte size prefix as shared_mac_data.

- [RLPx Framing](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/rlpx/framing.py): AES-256-CTR frame encryption. MAC: AES_encrypt(digest) XOR seed → feed to MAC.

- [RLPx Connection](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/rlpx/connection.py): Encrypted TCP with Snappy compression (msg_code >= 0x10 only).

- [eth/68 Protocol](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/eth/protocol.py): EthMsg enum with absolute codes (0x10+).

- [eth/68 Messages](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/eth/messages.py): Status, GetBlockHeaders, BlockHeaders, GetBlockBodies, BlockBodies, Transactions, and more.

- [snap/1 Protocol](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/snap/protocol.py): SnapMsg enum with relative codes (0-7). Convert via absolute_code().

- [snap/1 Messages](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/snap/messages.py): GetAccountRange/AccountRange, GetStorageRanges/StorageRanges, GetByteCodes/ByteCodes, GetTrieNodes/TrieNodes.

- [Protocol Registry](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/protocol_registry.py): Dynamic capability negotiation. Alphabetical sort, contiguous offsets.

- [Discovery v4](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/discv4/discovery.py): UDP Ping/Pong/FindNeighbours/Neighbours.

- [Kademlia Routing](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/discv4/routing.py): k-bucket routing table (k=16).

- [Full Sync](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/sync/full_sync.py): Head discovery via best_hash → header download → body download → block execution.

- [Snap Sync](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/networking/sync/snap_sync.py): 4-phase: account download → storage download → bytecode download → trie healing.

## JSON-RPC & Engine API (ethclient/rpc/)

- [RPC Server](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/rpc/server.py): FastAPI-based JSON-RPC 2.0 with batch support.

- [eth/net/web3 API](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/rpc/eth_api.py): 20+ methods — eth_blockNumber, eth_getBlockByNumber/Hash, eth_getBalance, eth_getTransactionCount, eth_getCode, eth_getStorageAt, eth_sendRawTransaction, eth_call, eth_estimateGas, eth_gasPrice, eth_maxPriorityFeePerGas, eth_feeHistory, eth_chainId, eth_syncing, eth_getTransactionByHash, eth_getTransactionReceipt, eth_getBlockTransactionCountByNumber/Hash, eth_getLogs, eth_getBlockReceipts, net_version, net_peerCount, net_listening, web3_clientVersion, web3_sha3.

- [Engine API](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/rpc/engine_api.py): V1/V2/V3 forkchoiceUpdated, getPayload, newPayload. Deterministic payload ID (SHA-256, geth-compatible). JWT auth on --engine-port (default 8551).

- [ZK API](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/rpc/zk_api.py): zk_verifyGroth16, zk_deployVerifier, zk_verifyOnChain. Supports both snarkjs and native format.

## Supported EIPs

- EIP-155: Replay protection via chain ID
- EIP-1559: Base fee, dynamic fees
- EIP-2718: Typed transaction envelope
- EIP-2929: Cold/warm storage access gas (2100 cold SLOAD, 100 warm)
- EIP-2930: Access list transactions (type 1)
- EIP-2200/3529: SSTORE gas rules with capped refunds
- EIP-2565: ModExp precompile gas cost
- EIP-152: BLAKE2f compression precompile
- EIP-196/197: BN128 elliptic curve add, mul, pairing
- EIP-4844: Blob transactions, KZG point evaluation precompile
- EIP-7702: Set EOA account code (Prague)

## CLI Usage

```
ethclient [OPTIONS]
ethclient l2 {init|start|prove|submit}

L2 Commands:
  l2 init --name NAME       Scaffold a new rollup project (l2.json + stf.py)
  l2 start --config PATH    Start rollup node
  l2 prove --batch N        Generate ZK proof for batch
  l2 submit --batch N       Submit proven batch to L1

L1 Node Options:
  --network {mainnet,sepolia,holesky}  Network (default: mainnet)
  --sync-mode {snap,full}              Sync mode (default: snap)
  --port PORT                          P2P port (default: 30303)
  --rpc-port PORT                      JSON-RPC port (default: 8545)
  --engine-port PORT                   Engine API port (default: 8551)
  --data-dir PATH                      Persistent storage directory
  --max-peers N                        Max peer connections (default: 25)
  --jwt-secret SECRET                  JWT secret for Engine API
  --log-level LEVEL                    Logging level (default: INFO)
```

## Installation

```bash
git clone https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient.git
cd py-ethclient
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"
```

Requires Python 3.12+ and snappy library (brew install snappy on macOS, apt install libsnappy-dev on Ubuntu).

## Dependencies

Runtime: pycryptodome (AES, SHA-256, RIPEMD-160), coincurve (secp256k1 ECDSA), eth-hash (Keccak-256), FastAPI + uvicorn (HTTP server), python-snappy (compression), py-ecc (BN128 curves), ckzg (KZG), lmdb (persistent storage).

Dev: pytest, pytest-asyncio.

## Examples (examples/)

19 self-contained examples organized into apps (business logic STF) and infra (production features):

### App Examples (examples/apps/) — 9 examples
- [l2_token.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/apps/l2_token.py): ERC20 token — mint, transfer, burn, admin control. STF pattern: basic CRUD with balance tracking.
- [l2_nameservice.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/apps/l2_nameservice.py): ENS-style domain register, update, transfer. STF pattern: ownership model.
- [l2_voting.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/apps/l2_voting.py): Governance — proposal creation, weighted voting, quorum-based finalization. STF pattern: time-based state transitions.
- [l2_rps_game.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/apps/l2_rps_game.py): Rock-paper-scissors with commit-reveal and betting. STF pattern: 2-phase protocol.
- [l2_dex.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/apps/l2_dex.py): Constant product AMM (x*y=k) with LP tokens, 0.3% fee, slippage checks. STF pattern: mathematical invariant verification.
- [l2_nft_marketplace.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/apps/l2_nft_marketplace.py): NFT mint/list/buy/transfer with 5% creator royalties and collections. STF pattern: nested state (owners, listings, collections).
- [l2_multisig.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/apps/l2_multisig.py): N-of-M multisig — propose, approve, auto-execute at threshold. STF pattern: multi-step workflow with authorization.
- [l2_escrow.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/apps/l2_escrow.py): 3-party escrow (buyer, seller, arbiter) with state machine. STF pattern: finite state machine (Created→Funded→Released/Refunded/Disputed).
- [l2_prediction_market.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/apps/l2_prediction_market.py): Prediction markets with oracle resolution and proportional payout. STF pattern: pool-based proportional math.

### Infra Examples (examples/infra/) — 10 examples
- [l2_rpc_server.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/infra/l2_rpc_server.py): L2 RPC server with 7 l2_* methods + APIKey, RateLimit, RequestSize middleware + /health, /ready, /metrics endpoints.
- [l2_persistent_state.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/infra/l2_persistent_state.py): LMDB persistence — batch creation → flush → crash simulation → WAL recovery → continued operation.
- [l2_da_providers.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/infra/l2_da_providers.py): DA layer comparison — LocalDAProvider (memory), CalldataDAProvider (EIP-1559), BlobDAProvider (EIP-4844 blob encode/decode + KZG).
- [l2_censorship_resistance.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/infra/l2_censorship_resistance.py): Force inclusion (sequencer censorship bypass) + escape hatch (L2 down → L1 fund recovery).
- [l2_multi_batch_ops.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/infra/l2_multi_batch_ops.py): 15-batch operations loop with prove/submit separation, batch chaining verification, query API demo.
- [l2_config_fullstack.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/infra/l2_config_fullstack.py): L2Config full-stack wiring — all backend options, NativeProver fallback, JSON config, CLI usage.
- [general_state_bridge.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/infra/general_state_bridge.py): L1↔L2 general state bridge end-to-end with Merkle proof.
- [bridge_relay_modes.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/infra/bridge_relay_modes.py): 5 relay modes comparison — EVM, Merkle, ZK, TinyDB, Direct.
- [zk_notebook_demo.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/infra/zk_notebook_demo.py): Groth16 end-to-end — circuit definition → trusted setup → prove → verify → EVM on-chain.
- [zk_note_settle.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/infra/zk_note_settle.py): ZK proof-based note settlement.

### Sepolia Live (examples/) — 2 examples
- [l2_sepolia_hello.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/l2_sepolia_hello.py): Sepolia testnet live deployment.
- [l2_sepolia_all.py](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/examples/l2_sepolia_all.py): Sepolia 4-app batch deployment.

## FAQ

Q: Can I build an application-specific rollup with py-ethclient?
A: Yes. Define your state transition logic as a Python function, and the framework handles sequencing, batching, Groth16 proving, and L1 verification. All 4 components (STF, DA, Prover, L1) are pluggable interfaces.

Q: Is there a Python Ethereum execution client?
A: Yes — py-ethclient is a fully functional Ethereum execution client written entirely in Python with 140+ EVM opcodes, RLPx P2P, and full+snap sync.

Q: Can py-ethclient sync with Ethereum mainnet?
A: Yes. Live-tested against Geth nodes on both Mainnet and Sepolia with full sync and snap sync.

Q: Can I use py-ethclient for ZK development?
A: Yes. Built-in Groth16 toolkit — define circuits in Python, generate proofs, verify natively or on the in-memory EVM. No circom/snarkjs needed.

Q: Is the ZK prover production-ready?
A: The prover is pure Python (py_ecc for BN128) — suited for education, prototyping, and small circuits (< 1000 constraints). Use snarkjs/rapidsnark for production proving, then verify with py-ethclient.

- [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): How to contribute
- [CLI Entry Point](https://github.com/tokamak-network/py-ethclient/blob/main/ethclient/main.py): Command-line interface
